records.in_frame: Hand holding the Arduview console close to camera, three fingers visible top and thumb below. Device oriented landscape, filling most of the frame. Clear resin shell with PCB and flex-ribbon cable visible through the sides. Display is active showing a retro pixel game: cyan/teal UI elements, heart icons top-right, score bar left, sprite character center-left, 'COIN 10' text rotated on right side. Green blurred foliage background. Board markings '308847 F-H 94V-0' and 'DT-D03...' visible through shell. No text overlay or watermark.

demonstrates.evidence: Display is fully lit with legible pixel game content — cyan UI bars, heart icons, sprite, 'COIN 10' text all clearly readable through the transparent pane; this is the focal point of the shot
Clear resin shell transmits view of PCB substrate, flex-ribbon cable, board markings ('94V-0', 'DT-D03...') visible along left and bottom edges of device
Active game on screen shows pixel-art sprite, score counter, hearts/lives HUD, coin counter — classic arcade game UI conventions fully legible
Device held in one hand, fitting comfortably between thumb and three fingers, immediately communicating palm/pocket size against human hand reference
Clean transparent enclosure and active glowing display read as a desirable display object, though shot is functional/in-use rather than styled for display
Resin shell edges visible and appear clean; clear casing shows solid construction; image resolution sufficient to see material clarity but not a dedicated quality detail shot

records.caption_hint: See every pixel — inside and on-screen.
records.selection_note: Best-in-class frame for the two load-bearing claims simultaneously (internals visible + display on_legible) with human-scale anchor; loses points only for portrait crop that cuts controls out of frame.

records.in_frame: Extreme close-up of the top portion of the Arduview device, angled slightly overhead. The transparent clear-resin enclosure is visible around the screen bezel. The OLED/LCD display is fully lit showing a pixel-font 'Game Over' screen with '13 Score' and '13 High' text in blue-white on black. A gold/amber flexible ribbon cable (FPC) is visible below the display, connecting into visible PCB components. The bottom edge of the shot reveals PCB traces and components through the clear shell. Background is a fabric or print with strawberry imagery — red fruit and green leaves. No people, no hands. No text overlay from operator; all text is rendered on the device display itself.

demonstrates.evidence: Display is fully illuminated with legible pixel-font game-over screen; blue-white pixels clearly lit on what reads as a transparent or translucent display pane inside the clear enclosure.
Clear resin shell is visibly transparent at the bezel and lower body; gold FPC ribbon cable and PCB traces/components are directly visible through the enclosure below the display.
Pixel-font 'Game Over / Score / High' screen is classic retro game UI; no D-pad or A/B buttons visible in this crop but the game state strongly implies retro gameplay.
Clear resin enclosure edges are visible around the display; finish looks clean and precise at macro scale, though some manufacturing micro-roughness is visible at the bezel edges.
Exposed FPC ribbon and visible PCB components through shell imply open/hackable architecture to a technically literate viewer, though no USB-C or explicit open framing is shown.

composition.background: Strawberry-print fabric or decorative surface — red/green organic pattern, informal and uncontrolled
composition.crop: Tight macro crop on upper device body and display; bottom ~30% of device and all controls cut off; no full-device silhouette

records.caption_hint: Game over — but you can see exactly what just happened, right through the board.
records.selection_note: Strong evidence for both load-bearing claims (see-through display on-legible + internals visible through shell); crop loses controls and scale context — best used as a detail/supporting shot, not hero.

records.in_frame: Tight crop of the upper portion of the Arduview console. The transparent clear-resin enclosure fills the frame. The display is active, showing a pixel-art 'Game Over / 13 Score / 13 High' screen in bright cyan-blue on a dark background. Below the display, through the clear shell, the PCB is visible: a large black IC chip, flex ribbon cable labeled UT-0205 / 94V-0, and board traces/components. Controls and lower body are out of frame. Background is a color-blocked flat-lay surface in olive/yellow-green, blue, and purple/lavender quadrants.

demonstrates.evidence: Display is clearly lit and legible — cyan pixel-art 'Game Over' text and score readout visible on the transparent pane; display module seated inside clear resin shell is fully readable
Through the clear resin shell below the display, a large black IC, flex ribbon cable, SMD components, and PCB traces are all plainly visible to the naked eye
Pixel-art bitmap font 'Game Over' screen with score tally visible on display confirms retro game running; no D-pad/buttons in frame
Clear resin enclosure edges are visible at frame borders; molded corners appear clean and even; detail-macro framing reveals no visible flash or parting-line defects in the resin
Color-blocked flat-lay background and close macro framing suggest deliberate product-object presentation, implying display/collectible intent, but no explicit display context
Flex ribbon cable with manufacturer marking (UT-0205 / 94V-0) and exposed PCB components visible through shell suggest open/hackable construction, but no Arduino or USB-C framing in shot

composition.background: Flat-lay color-blocked surface: olive/yellow-green top-left, blue top-right, purple/lavender bottom-left, sage/muted green bottom-right
composition.crop: Tight upper-body crop; lower third of device (controls, bottom edge) cut off; display and PCB internals centered

records.caption_hint: The screen is on. The board is visible. Nothing is hidden.
records.selection_note: Best single shot to simultaneously prove both load-bearing claims — on_legible display + PCB internals through shell — in one tight frame; use as anchor for transparency+gaming story.

records.in_frame: Close-cropped top-down view of the Arduview console upper-left quadrant. Left half shows the transparent OLED/display panel glowing cyan-blue with pixel-font text reading 'game over / 13 Score / 13 High' inside a rectangular border. Right half shows the clear resin shell with the PCB fully visible through it: a large black QFP microcontroller, FPC ribbon cable connecting to the display, gold PCB traces, component pads, two dome buttons (top-right), one square tactile button (bottom-right), and silkscreen text 'ArduView' on the board. Colorful pastel striped surface (blue, green, yellow, purple bands) beneath the device. No hand, no cable, no full device outline visible — partial crop of the top edge.

demonstrates.evidence: Display pane is actively glowing cyan-blue pixel text ('game over', score values) on a transparent substrate; PCB and FPC ribbon are visible through and behind the display panel itself.
Clear resin shell shows full PCB: QFP chip, FPC ribbon, gold traces, solder pads, button domes, and 'ArduView' silkscreen all legible through the enclosure.
Pixel-font 'game over' screen with score readout confirms game software running; no D-pad visible in this crop.
QFP microcontroller, FPC connector, and exposed PCB layout visible through shell reinforce open/hackable hardware framing; no USB-C visible in this crop.
Resin edges and shell corners appear clean and well-molded in the visible crop; macro detail reveals some minor assembly roughness at display bezel edges.
Aesthetic contrast of glowing cyan display against the visible PCB internals and pastel background reads as a designed, display-worthy object.

composition.background: Pastel striped surface — horizontal bands of blue, sage green, yellow-green, and purple
composition.crop: Tight macro crop of upper-left device quadrant; device edges partially cut off on top and right; no full silhouette

records.caption_hint: Game over — and you can see exactly why: every component that drove it, right through the shell.
records.selection_note: Best single frame for dual load-bearing claims: on_legible display state plus full PCB transparency in one tight crop; weak on pocket_scale and retro controls due to partial composition.

records.in_frame: Close-cropped top-down view of the Arduview console, showing the left half of the device. The transparent resin shell is clearly visible with the PCB and internal components fully exposed beneath it. The small OLED-style display is active, showing cyan pixel-font text reading 'game over / 13 Score / 13 High' with a rectangular UI border — legible game UI. A flex cable ribbon runs from the display module to the main chip. The microcontroller (large QFP chip), circuit traces, and two round tactile buttons on the right side are all visible through the shell. The 'ArduView' logo is etched or printed on the PCB itself. The unit rests on a multicolor geometric background (blue, olive, lavender, pink panels). No USB-C port, D-pad, or A/B face buttons are in frame — the shot is cropped to the top portion of the device.

demonstrates.evidence: Display is lit with cyan pixel-font 'game over / 13 Score / 13 High' text clearly rendered on the transparent pane; background PCB is visible through and around the display panel itself
Clear resin shell shows large QFP microcontroller, circuit traces, flex ribbon cable, and button contacts fully visible through the enclosure without obstruction
Display shows a 'game over' score screen in pixel/bitmap font with rectangular UI border — classic retro game UI; two round tactile buttons visible on right edge
Resin shell edges are visible and appear clean; internal component layout is tidy; flex cable and PCB finish look intentional rather than raw prototype — but crop limits full-device assessment
The combination of lit display + visible internals in a tight macro gives a premium display-object feel, but no explicit display/shelf context is staged

composition.background: Multicolor geometric flat-lay surface — four color blocks (blue, olive/yellow-green, lavender, pink) arranged in quadrants
composition.crop: Tight detail crop showing approximately the top 60% of the device; USB-C port, D-pad, and lower A/B buttons are out of frame

records.caption_hint: Game over — and you can see every component that ran it.
records.selection_note: Best available proof-shot for transparency_internals_visible + see_through_display_on together at macro fidelity; crop hides controls so weak on pocket_scale and retro_controls — pair with a full-device shot in any sequence.

gap_list.reason: no strong+clean asset — reshoot or source
